judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 answered better: it hits all four expectations, whereas Report 1 misses the same-commit doc-update principle (c) and the recommendation to drop the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the code (d).

report_1.evidence: New provider calls (e.g. a `refund(...)`) should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`. ... Two places talk to the provider directly, each constructing their own `chargekit.Client`: - `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` ... - `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` ... `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` also imports from `chargekit`, but only the exception classes — this is the explicitly sanctioned boundary exception ... not a violation.

report_2.evidence: New provider calls (e.g. a `refund(...)`) belong in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — constructs its own `chargekit.Client(...)` and calls `client.charge(...)` directly ... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` — constructs its own `chargekit.Client(...)` and calls `.charge(...)` directly ... `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6` — imports only `chargekit.errors` exception types; the doc explicitly sanctions this as the one exception.

report_1.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` in `backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-28` has **6** fields — it adds `external_ref`. So the doc's "exactly" is wrong; `external_ref` is missing.

report_2.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`models/invoice.py:16-28`) also includes `external_ref`, which the charge route populates (`invoices.py:17`) and which was added by migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`. So the doc's "exactly" is wrong — it omits `external_ref`.

doc/code drift is a bug, not a someday-cleanup. The document should be corrected to match reality ... in the **same commit** as the change that touches these surfaces — i.e., as part of the refund work
rather than hard-coding a count that silently goes stale ... I'd drop the "4" and either list the handled types as they appear in `webhooks.py` or point at `webhooks.py` as the source of truth.
